item.text: I’ll inspect the repository instructions and existing files, then derive the functional-graph DP, implement `solution.py`, and validate it against samples plus brute force on small cases.

The graph decomposes into directed cycles with in-trees. Every cycle must have one common value, while each tree contributes a prefix-sum DP. I’m implementing this in \(O(NM)\) time with compact integer arrays so the worst case does not incur Python-object memory blowups.

item.command:
/bin/zsh -lc "python3 solution.py << 'EOF'
3 3
2 1 1
EOF
python3 solution.py << 'EOF'
4 9
1 1 1 1
EOF
python3 solution.py << 'EOF'
10 5
9 4 5 5 4 2 1 5 7 2
EOF"

/bin/zsh -lc "python3 solution.py << 'EOF'
3 3
2 1 1
EOF
python3 solution.py << 'EOF'
4 9
1 1 1 1
EOF
python3 solution.py << 'EOF'
10 5
9 4 5 5 4 2 1 5 7 2
EOF"

/bin/zsh -lc "python3 - << 'PY'
import itertools, random, subprocess, sys
random.seed(1)
for n in range(1, 7):
    for _ in range(30):
        m = random.randint(1, 4)
        a = [random.randrange(n) for _ in range(n)]
        expected = 0
        for x in itertools.product(range(1, m + 1), repeat=n):
            expected += all(x[i] <= x[a[i]] for i in range(n))
        inp = f'{n} {m}\\n' + ' '.join(str(v + 1) for v in a) + '\\n'
        got = int(subprocess.check_output([sys.executable, 'solution.py'], input=inp.encode()))
        if got "'!= expected:
            raise SystemExit(f'"'mismatch: {n=} {m=} {a=} {expected=} {got=}')
print('random brute-force checks passed')

n = m = 2025
inp = f'{n} {m}\\n' + ' '.join(str((i + 1) % n + 1) for i in range(n)) + '\\n'
subprocess.run([sys.executable, 'solution.py'], input=inp.encode(), stdout=subprocess.DEVNULL, check=True)
print('maximum-size cycle check passed')
PY"

item.text:
Implemented the complete solution in [solution.py](/Users/james/Library/Mobile%20Documents/com~apple~CloudDocs/Code/live-code-bench/LiveCodeBench/native-codex-gpt-5.6-sol-ultrafast-high-q1-5-2026-07-13_145017/q1_abc387_f/solution.py).

It uses functional-graph cycle decomposition and tree DP:

- Time: \(O(NM)\)
- Memory: \(O(NM)\) worst case, using compact integer arrays

Validated against all samples, randomized brute-force cases, and maximum-size inputs.
